_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-11 Hypothermia/Frostbite (Revised 03/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-11: Hypothermia/Frostbite — MDHHS Approved 3/17/25, Reviewed 2025

sections.title: Hypothermia — General Management
points: If the patient is in cardiac arrest, follow the Adult or Pediatric Cardiac Arrest protocol as applicable
Move the patient to a warm, dry place and handle gently
Remove wet clothing and wrap the patient in dry blankets
If the patient can be evacuated to a warm ambulance within 30 minutes, leave clothing in place, wrap in a vapor and wind barrier, and remove wet clothing once the patient is warm
If temperature is less than 30°C / 86°F: handle gently and transport immediately

sections.title: Rewarming and Support
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-11: Hypothermia — Rewarming and Support
points: If temperature is greater than 30°C / 86°F, or if the patient is shivering and conscious, place heat packs in the axillae and on the torso
Administer oxygen; warm and humidify oxygen if a commercial device is available
Check blood glucose for altered mental status and treat per Altered Mental Status protocol; assess for other causes of altered mental status
If hypotensive, refer to the Shock protocol; warm IV fluids if a commercial warming device is available
If alert, provide warm non-caffeinated beverages by mouth slowly

sections.title: Frostbite
points: Remove wet or constricting clothing and keep the affected area dry and protected from wind
Do not thaw frostbitten tissue if there is a possibility of refreezing or if the patient must walk to transportation
Cover the affected area with a light clean cloth
Do not rub the area and do not break blisters
Keep the patient warm and support and elevate the affected area
Refer to the Pain Management protocol as needed

key_drugs.name: Warmed IV Fluid (NS or LR)
key_drugs.dose: Per Shock protocol when hypotensive; warm IV fluids if a commercial warming device is available
key_drugs.indication: Hypothermia with hypotension requiring fluid support
key_drugs.contraindications: Fluid overload; use caution with cardiac history
key_drugs.side_effects: Pulmonary edema if over-administered; temperature-related dysrhythmias if given too cold
key_drugs.notes: Use warmed fluids when a commercial warming device is available. Handle the patient gently when establishing access.

deterioration_flags: Temperature less than 30°C / 86°F — gentle handling and immediate transport
Cardiac arrest in hypothermia — follow Adult or Pediatric Cardiac Arrest protocol
Altered mental status — check blood glucose and assess for causes other than hypothermia
Frostbite with risk of refreezing or patient must walk to transport — do NOT thaw in the field; protect tissue and transport
scope_notes: Environmental removal, passive rewarming, blankets, vapor/wind barrier wrapping, gentle handling: all levels
Warmed humidified oxygen: all levels when equipment available
IV/IO access and warmed fluid administration for hypotension: Specialist skill
Pain management for frostbite: refer to Pain Management-Procedure Protocol
